summary.intent: Iterate on PR #529 (branch: glistening-amazonsaurus) until Greptile gives a 5/5 confidence score with zero unresolved comments by fixing all flagged issues.
summary.outcome: Fixed both P1 issues flagged by Greptile (streaming retry storm and URL encoding bug), added a test, passed all 71 tests with clean clippy/fmt, and committed+pushed the changes. Greptile re-review was triggered but final score not yet confirmed.

repo: The msteams crate has streaming, outbound, and graph modules for MS Teams integration, living under crates/msteams/src/
Greptile is used as an automated code review gate on PRs; the workflow targets 5/5 confidence with zero unresolved threads
The project uses worktrees under /Users/penso/.superset/worktrees/moltis/ for branch isolation

code.finding: StreamSession had no flag to track initial post failure; without it, ready_for_initial_post() returns true on every delta after a failed post, causing a retry storm. Fixed by adding initial_post_failed bool field and mark_initial_post_failed() method.

When the initial HTTP post fails, activity_id stays None so ready_for_initial_post() re-triggers on every subsequent LLM streaming token. Fixed by calling mark_initial_post_failed() in the error path.
search_messages constructed the URL by interpolating raw strings, breaking on & characters in query or user parameters. Fixed by switching to reqwest's .query() builder for proper percent-encoding.

workflow: Greptile review state must be polled via check runs, review comments, and issue comments separately — confidence score location is not uniform
cargo fmt must be run explicitly after edits; clippy and fmt are separate checks and both should be clean before committing
The greploop pattern: read Greptile output → identify P1s → fix → test → fmt/clippy → commit → push → re-trigger review

friction: Greptile confidence score and unresolved threads required querying multiple separate GitHub API endpoints (check runs, review comments, issue comments) — no single source of truth
cargo fmt produced a diff after manual edits, requiring a separate format pass before committing
open_items: Greptile re-review not yet confirmed to reach 5/5 — final score pending after the push
The streaming retry storm fix uses a boolean flag; a more robust approach might track failure reason or use an enum state machine
Other Greptile comments besides the 2 P1s may exist and were not all addressed in this session
